title: Intensely Roasted Vietnamese Tofu Skewers
description: A crispy and flavorful Vietnamese dish featuring roasted tofu skewers, umeboshi, and a blend of traditional spices, all wrapped in a ti-leaf for a unique presentation.
instructions: Preheat the robata grill to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Whisk together umeboshi paste, soy sauce, rice vinegar, brown sugar, grated ginger, and garlic in a large bowl.
Add the cubed tofu to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Thread the marinated tofu onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Wrap each skewer in a ti leaf, securing with a toothpick if necessary.
Brush the ti leaves with sesame oil and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Grill the skewers for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until the tofu is crispy and golden brown.
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with chopped scallions.
Let the skewers rest for a few minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
